observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to locate the pertinent documents relative to the 11 cows for verification before adjustments could be made in the books of the LGU. The Management may also consider to request for relief from accountability following the procedures set forth in 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit, if after exerting effort, sufficient data needed for the derecognition of the breeding stocks could not be located.
observations.managementAction: Efforts are being done by Management to locate the needed documents for Breeding Stocks.
observations.reason: Breeding Stocks account still had a balance of ₱110,000.00 in the financial statements as of December 31, 2021, hence, reiterated in this report under Observation No. 8, pages 38-39.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Survey Municipal Treasurer, to facilitate the titling of land to protect the interest of the LGU for any adverse claims by third parties.
observations.managementAction: Made a follow up on the application for approval.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, only seven of the 15 parcels of land of the LGU had Transfer Certificate of Title, thus, reiterated in this report under Observation No. 11, pages 44-45.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate the documents pertaining to the non-moving accounts in order to substantiate its balances and to enforce collections. In case of possibility of non-collection or no longer be accounted or reconciled, the Management may request for write-off of the dormant accounts to clean the accounts subject to the requirements or procedures embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Efforts are being done by Management to locate the needed documents for dormant accounts.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, the amount of ₱613,635.81 remained dormant accounts; hence, reiterated in this report under Observation No. 7, pages 37-38.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Head of the Disposal Committee to prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal procedures in the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations to avoid further deterioration and loss of its salvage value and generate additional funds from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The unserviceable properties were not yet disposed, thus, reiterated in this report under Observation No. 9, pages 39-41.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the members of the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat, to strictly adhere to Sections 48.2 and 54.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts in order to attain the most advantageous terms for the LGU.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Some procurements of goods were procured on a piecemeal basis and resorted to alternative mode of procurement instead of public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Punong Barangays to operate their MRF in order to collect recyclable wastes which shall be converted to bricks using the MRF Machineries that are useful to the people and to the environment.
observations.managementAction: Ten of the 15 barangay MRFs are operating already. The municipality is assisting the remaining five barangays to make the MRFS operational.
observations.reason: Only 10 out of 15 barangays of the LGU have operational MRFs. The MRF machineries were not completely operational, thus, reiterated in this report under Observation No. 21, pages 59-60.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management consider to provide longer solution for the disposal of wastes through sanitary landfill.

We also recommended that Management enjoin the municipal and barangay officials in the enforcement of segregation of wastes at the source.
observations.managementAction:
The municipality was allowed by Metro Clark Management Corporation to dispose waste through Cash upon disposal or Cash Basis.

No sanitary landfill was maintained by the LGU and the MRF machineries were not completely operational.
observations.reason: No sanitary landfill was maintained by the LGU and the MRF machineries were not completely operational, thus, reiterated in this report under Observation No. 21, pages 59-60.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
depreciate the depreciable components of the road networks system at cost
and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts to
present the correct amount of Road Networks and Depreciation –
Infrastructure Assets accounts in the financial statements.
1.7 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the
adjusting journal entries to revert the depreciation expense recognized for
the fully depreciated assets in the amount of ₱ 3,102,623.29.

2.8 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the
affected accounts in the financial statements and to observe the proper usage
of accounts in recording financial transactions.

3.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare adjusting journal entries relative to the misclassification of account
presented in the financial statements.

4.10 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to
charge to the appropriate budgetary account by considering the actual nature of
the transaction.
4.11 Likewise, we recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that transactions are recorded based on the actual
nature of the transaction and not on the account shown in the obligation
request to present the correct balances of accounts presented in the financial
statements.

5.5 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
analyse and evaluate the unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for
more than two years of which no actual claims were made and to revert to the
government equity of the General Fund and Special Education Fund which
shall be made available for appropriation.

6.5 The Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team in her letter-reply that her
office is exerting efforts to inquire with the source agencies regarding the complete
details of the funds in order to return to the source agencies the unutilized funds.

7.5 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
locate the documents pertaining to the non-moving accounts in order to
substantiate its balances and to enforce collections. In case of possibility of
non-collection or could no longer be accounted or reconciled, the
Management, through the Municipal Accountant, may request for write-off
of the dormant accounts to clean the accounts.

8.5 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to
exert effort to locate the pertinent documents relative to the 11 cows for
verification before adjustments could be made in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the description of account as prescribed in the Chart of Accounts, Volume III under GAM for LGUs and reclassify the expense account in the recording of the construction of Barangay MRF to Other Structures account to present the correct balances of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the refund of overpayment of salaries and wages of some laborers amounting to ₱39,100.00.
observations.managementAction:
The Management reclassified the accounts from Subsidy to Other LGUs account to Other Structures account under JEV No. 100-21-07-0020 dated July 30, 2021.

Refunded under OR No. 7937406 dated July 28, 2021. Analysis of Collections and Deposits showed that the overpayment was already refunded under OR No. 7937406 dated July 28, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the road components recorded as Other Infrastructure to Road Network account and reclassify the Other Structures account to Road Networks account.

We likewise recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects in order to present the correct balances of the PPE and Depreciation Expense accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Effected the adjusting entries under JEV No. 100-21-04-0012 dated April 16, 2021. The necessary adjusting entries were effected by Management.

The depreciation costs were provided in the same Journal Entry Voucher. The depreciation costs were provided for the reclassified assets.
observations.reason: The complete description and cost segregation of the said component for road projects were not yet provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the outstanding/stale check reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statement and effect adjustments in the books of accounts.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the checks and to follow the procedures prescribed to ensure correctness of recording of adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The books were adjusted for the amount of stale checks under JEV 100-21-01-0002 dated January 29, 2021.

Notices were sent and stale checks were replaced upon request of payees. Verification of entries revealed that stale checks were reverted to Cash in Bank account and was reclassified as Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that BAC members require the members of the BAC to consider the SRP in the procurement of emergency goods and services as set forth in the GPPB. We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC members to strictly follow the procedures for the procurement of goods and services as set forth in GPPB in order to promote transparency and to obtain the better terms for the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Management observed the audit recommendation of considering the SRP of procurement of essential medicines and medical supplies.

The required documents from the supplier for the purchase of supplies were submitted. The Management submitted the required documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
Some of the procurements of goods were not in accordance with GPPB issuances.

The revised APP changes was submitted. Post audit of accounts revealed that there were procured that are not incorporated in the APP of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Require the BAC members to ensure that all PPAs are already procured in accordance with the APP of the LGU and to revise the APP for any changes to the proposed PPAs; and
2. Instruct the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant to submit the mandatory documents from the supplier to support the payment of the transactions.
observations.managementAction:
The required documents from the supplier were submitted.

The revised APP for changes was submitted.
observations.reason: The revised APP changes was submitted. Post audit of accounts revealed that there were procured that are not incorporated in the APP of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that PPAs charged to the 20% DF partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic, development and environmental management outcome.
observations.managementAction: The fund intended for certified seeds was realigned to the Construction of One Stop Shop Building.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the aforementioned lacking documents to properly substantiate the propriety and validity of the recorded transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking documents. The lacking documents required were properly submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort more effort to locate the documents pertaining to the non-moving accounts in order to substantiate its balances and to enforce collections. In case of the possibility of non-collection, the agency may request for write-off of the dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures and requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has exerted effort to locate documents to substantiate its balance so that collections can be enforced.
observations.reason: The dormant accounts were still not provided with complete documentation; hence, reiterated under observation no. 5, pages 36-38.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to create disposal committee to take care of the unserviceable properties and to dispose said items in accordance with the requirements and procedures of Section 76 and 79 of PD 1445 to avoid further deterioration and loss of its salvage value.
observations.managementAction: A Disposal Committee was already created by the Local Chief Executive but the disposal of unserviceable properties did not push through in view of this COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Though a disposal committee was created, the unserviceable properties were not disposed; hence, reiterated under Observation No. 7, pages 39-41.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to exert more effort to locate the pertinent documents relative to the 11 cows for verification and shall serve as basis in the recording of adjustments in the book of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculture Officer is in the process of locating the documents related to Breeding stocks.
observations.reason: Pertinent data needed were not yet located; thus, reiterated under Observation No. 3, pages 33-35.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Provide or develop its own engineered sanitary landfill for disposal of its residual non-recyclable solid wastes;


Ensure the establishment of MRF in every barangay or cluster barangays in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003; and

Enjoin the municipal and barangay officials to enforce the segregation of wastes in their barangays in order to provide a pollution and health hazard-free environment.
observations.managementAction: Committee meetings of SB members on the Environment Code of the municipality which will answer issues on solid waste management were done. The next step will be public hearing. Subsidies to barangays for the establishment of barangay MRFs were allotted. Some barangay MRFs are already operating.
observations.reason:
No engineered sanitary landfill was developed by the LGU; hence, reiterated under Observation No. 14, pages 52-53.

Fully Implemented

The LGU constructed MRF for its barangays. 

Fully Implemented

The construction of Barangay MRF is the LGU’s way in enjoining the barangay officials to address the problems on solid waste management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the description of account as prescribed in the Chart of Accounts, Volume III under GAM for LGUs and reclassify the expense account in the recording of the construction of Barangay MRF to Other Structures account to present the correct balances of the accounts presented in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the refund of overpayment of salaries and wages of some laborers amounting to ₱39,100.00.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the road components recorded as Other Infrastructure to Road Network account and to provide depreciation costs after reclassification of the completed projects in order to present the correct balances of the PPE and Depreciation Expense accounts in the financial statements.
We likewise recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to locate the pertinent documents relative to the 11 cows for verification before adjustments could be made in the books of the LGU. The Management may also consider to request for relief from accountability following the procedures set forth in 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit, if after exerting effort, sufficient data needed for the derecognition of the breeding stocks could not be located.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to facilitate the titling of land to protect the interest of the LGU for any adverse claims by third parties.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate the documents pertaining to the non-moving accounts in order to substantiate its balances and to enforce collections. In case of possibility of non-collection or no longer be accounted or reconciled, the Management may request for write-off of the dormant accounts to clean the accounts subject to the requirements or procedures embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the outstanding/stale check reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statement and effect adjustments in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the checks and to follow the procedures prescribed to ensure correctness of recording of adjustment in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Head of the Disposal Committee to prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal procedures in the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations to avoid further deterioration and loss of its salvage value and generate additional funds from the sale thereof.

We recommended that Management require the members of the BAC to consider the SRP in the procurement of emergency essential medicines and medical devices due as prescribed under Department Memorandum No. 2020-0345 of DOH dated August 10, 2020.
We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC members to strictly follow the procedures for the procurement of goods and services as set forth in GPPB in the procurement process in order to promote transparency and to obtain the better terms for the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC members to ensure that all PPAs are procured in accordance with the APP of the LGU and to revise the APP for any changes to the proposed PPAs; and
Instruct the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant to submit the mandatory documentary requirements from the supplier to support the payment of the transactions.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that PPAs charged to the 20% DF partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic, development and environmental management outcome.

We recommended that Management require the members of the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat, to strictly adhere to Sections 48.2 and 54.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts in order to attain the most advantageous terms for the LGU.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the aforementioned lacking documents to properly substantiate the propriety and validity of the recorded transactions.

We recommended that Management require the Punong Barangays to operate their MRF in order to collect recyclable wastes which shall converted to bricks using the MRF Machineries that are useful to the people and to the environment.

We recommended that the Management consider to provide longer solution for the disposal of wastes through sanitary landfill. We also recommended that Management enjoin the municipal and barangay officials in the enforcement of segregation of wastes at source.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate reclassification of the repairs and maintenance expense account recorded as Local Road Networks account in order to reflect the correct amount of the said account recorded in the books of the Municipality:

Require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation cost for Local Road Networks account in accordance with IPSAS 17; and

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has reclassified the ₱15,523,895.58 recorded in the Local Road Networks account in order to reflect the correct amount of the account.

The Municipal Accountant provided the corresponding depreciation cost for each component. 

The Municipal Engineer has provided the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer complete description of road projects
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant reflected the adjusting entries as per JEV No. 100-20-10-36 dated October 30, 2020.


Fully Implemented

Not Implemented

As per the report of physical count of local road network, description and cost segregation of road components for road projects were not provided; hence, reiterated in Observation No. 1, pages 28-30.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management thru the initiative of the Municipal Mayor and enactment of SB Resolution cause the transfer of the cost or value of properties of the implemented buildings to the end-users or barangays concerned to comply with the provisions of IPSAS 17 to be supported with complete documentation to provide proper recording in the books of the LGU and the recipient barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Property Plant and Equipment in the amount of  ₱604,709.40 were transferred by the Municipality to the different barangays as per SB Resolution No.54, Series of 2020
observations.reason: Through SB Resolution No. 54-2020, said PPEs were transferred to the Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances particularly those outstanding for a long period of time in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Advances to Officers and Employees amounting ₱167,000.00 were already liquidated.
observations.reason: The accountable officer refunded the unliquidated cash advance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that expenditures and obligations incurred during a fiscal year shall be taken up in the accounts of that year. 



We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all valid claims not paid during the year as Account Payable at the end year for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Only expenditures incurred in the current year were charged in the same year.
observations.reason:
The Management stopped the practice of charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation.


Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should give priority in implementation of projects under the 20% development fund in order to attain maximum benefits for which the fund was intended as stated in the guidelines of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We also recommended that Management prepare and implement projects chargeable to the 20% DF in the nature of investment and capital expenditures to contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic, development and environmental management outcome.
observations.managementAction: The remaining projects in the 20% Development Fund for 2019 were completed in 2020.
observations.reason:
PPAs under 20% DF were implemented. 



Not Implemented

Purchase of certified seeds which was charged against the 20% DF did not partake in the nature of investment and capital expenditure; hence, reiterated under Observation No. 10 pages 46-47.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Ensure that all procurements are within the approved budget and only projects/procurement that are included in the APP shall be undertaken; and

Require the BAC Technical Working Group (TWG), BAC Secretariat and BAC Chairman to submit a written justifications on the contract awarded to the bidder considered as non-responsive due to non-disclosure of on-going projects on the date of bid submission and non-compliance with the minimum major equipment as required, and why the said project were not included in the APP for further evaluation by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
The Supplemental AIP and Supplemental APP were already submitted 


Justification for the awarding of contract to the bidder was also submitted
observations.reason:
PPAs are appropriately budgeted. 

Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant to submit copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents to the COA auditor within the prescribed period of five (5) days as required under COA Circular No. 2009-01 for a systematic and effective review process to come up with timely and relevant audit results.
observations.managementAction: The copies of the perfected contracts and supporting documents were submitted to the Office of the Auditor
observations.reason: Deadline of submission of perfected contracts was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should give priority in implementing projects under the GAD plan and programs to attain maximum fulfilment of its objectives to address the specific gender issues and concerns affecting the welfare and full development of women in accordance with the above cited Joint Circular No. 2013-001.
observations.managementAction: The ₱2,032,999.75 were realigned to other projects/programs/activities which were also implemented as of year end
observations.reason: The unimplemented PPAs streamlined to GAD budget was due to health and safety risks imposed by COVID-19.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to continue and intensify their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOP’s and notice of delinquency to taxpayers and forming committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes; and




If circumstances warrant, impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes to satisfy taxes, penalties and costs with the following:

Seizure of personal property of the taxpayer and sale thereof at public auction;

File civil suit with the proper court; and

Levy on real property and sale of delinquent property or a usable portion thereof at public auction.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasury Office is continuously exerting efforts to collect the real property taxes.

Sent notice of delinquencies and continuous follow up is being undertaken.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office are continuously sending Notice of Delinquencies to concerned taxpayers. Tarpaulins are posted to conspicuous place to remind the residents of their duty to pay RPT. Also, the pandemic hindered the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to campaign well.


Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Direct the supplier to install the machineries in the specified site. In case the supplier/contractor refuse to comply, the agency may suspend the contract as per contract of agreement and issue notice of blacklisting of the said supplier;

Submit a written justification relative to the delivered machineries by the supplier which did not conform to some of the Technical Specification as stipulated in the Schedule of Requirements and in the Purchase Request but were inspected and accepted on November 29, 2018 by Management and paid on February 21, 2019, for evaluation by the Audit Team; and

Require the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasury Office to submit the lacking documents needed to support the payment made.
observations.managementAction:
The supplier already installed the machineries


The supplier promised to correct the machineries which did not conform to some of the Technical Specifications


The lacking documents were submitted.
observations.reason:
The supplier installed the machineries at the MRF site of the LGU. 

Fully Implemented 




Fully Implemented

Lacking documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate
reclassification of the repairs and maintenance expense account
recorded as Local Road Networks account in order to reflect the
correct amount of the said account recorded in the books of the
Municipality;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation cost
for Local Road Networks account in accordance with IPSAS 17;
and
c. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer the complete description
and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Management thru the initiative of the
Municipal Mayor and enactment of SB Resolution cause the transfer of the
cost or value of properties of the implemented buildings to the end-users or
barangays concerned to comply with the provisions of IPSAS 17 to be
supported with complete documentation to provide proper recording in the
books of the LGU and the recipient barangays.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate liquidation of all outstanding
cash advances particularly those outstanding for a long period of time in
accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to locate the documents pertaining
to the non-moving accounts in order to substantiate its balances and to enforce
collections. In case of the possibility of non-collection, the agency may request
for write-off of the dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures and
requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016, for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create disposal
committee to take care of the unserviceable properties and to dispose said
items in accordance with the requirements and procedures of Sections 76 and
79 of PD 1445 to avoid further deterioration and loss of its salvage value.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the
Municipal Agriculture Officer to exert more effort to locate the pertinent
documents relative to the 11 cows for verification and shall serve as basis in
the recording of adjustments in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that expenditures and obligations
incurred during a fiscal year shall be taken up in the accounts of that year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all valid claims
not paid during the year as Accounts Payable at the end year for fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management should give priority in the
implementation of projects under the 20% development fund in order to attain
maximum benefits for which the fund was intended as stated in the guidelines
of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. We also
recommended that Management prepare and implement projects chargeable
to the 20% DF in the nature of investment and capital expenditures to
contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic, development and
environmental management outcome.

We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that all procurements are within the approved budget
and only projects/procurement that are included in the APP
shall be undertaken; and
b. Require the BAC Technical Working Group (TWG), BAC
Secretariat and BAC Chairman to submit a written
justifications on the contract awarded to the bidder considered
as non-responsive due to non-disclosure of on-going projects on
the date of bid submission and non-compliance with the
minimum major equipment as required, and why the said
projects were not included in the APP for further evaluation by
the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant to
submit copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents to the COA
auditor within the prescribed period of five (5) days as required under COA
Circular No. 2009-01 for a systematic and effective review process to come up
with timely and relevant audit results.

We recommended that Management should give priority in
implementing projects under the GAD plan and programs to attain maximum
fulfilment of its objectives to address the specific gender issues and concerns
affecting the welfare and full development of women in accordance with the
above cited Joint Circular No. 2013-001.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Provide or develop its own engineered sanitary landfill for disposal
of its residual non-recyclable solid wastes;
b. Ensure the establishment of MRF in every barangay or cluster
barangays in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003; and
c. Enjoin the municipal and barangay officials to enforce the
segregation of wastes in their barangays in order to provide a
pollution and health hazard-free environment.

We recommended that Management effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts of the Municipality to present the correct amount of Loans Payable - Domestic account and the expenses in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant effected the necessary adjusting entries in its books as per JEV No. 100-19-02-003 and 100-19-02-004

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:
a. Secure immediately property insurance for the non-insured government assets; and
b. Submit the updated Property Inventory Form (PIF) as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage using the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 for all insurable assets not later than April 30 of each year.
observations.managementAction: The management insured some of its vital assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means for speedy titling of land and secure Certificate of Title in order to protect the interest of the Municipality for any adverse claims by third parties.
observations.managementAction: The management is in the process of securing titles for the five parcels of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents on terminal leave benefit claims before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured the completeness of supporting documents on terminal leave benefits before effecting payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the rules and regulations before granting any allowances. Further, we recommended the Management to stop giving monthly transportation allowance to officers and Barangay Presidents of the Federation of Senior Citizen Association considering that said expenditure is not included in the provision as provided under Section 3, Article 21, Rule VI of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9994 and to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management stopped giving monthly transportation allowance to Barangay Presidents of the Federation of Senior Citizens.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers (DVs) as listed in Annex G in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Disbursement Vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the drivers concerned and agency personnel of the Municipality to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels and submit the same to the Auditor for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submits Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that every Purchase Order is duly filled up with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in complying with the terms and conditions thereof in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Purchase Orders are now properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:
a. Comply with Government Procurement Reform Act, which states that all procurement should be within the approved budget and only projects/procurement included in the APP shall be undertaken;
b. Limit expenditures to what is appropriated in the budget to avoid utilizing appropriations intended for other programs and or activities and submit explanation/justification on why the monoblock chairs were purchased despite the non-inclusion in the APP as pursuant to Section 7, Article II of RA 9184; and
c. Carefully plan its APP in order to minimize or avoid deviations from the approved APP and to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management see to it that all of its expenses are in accordance with its APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 15, 2009 and classify the expenses to proper expenses account. We also recommended to require the Municipal Budget Officer to charge/obligate expenses to its proper account and to stop charging expenses which are not aligned with the appropriation corresponding to its expense account for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management properly charged the correct accounts affected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 54.1 of Republic Act 9184 and Administrative Order No. 17 to ensure that the procurement of goods and services are obtained in the process which are most advantageous to the Municipality; and
b.Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply strictly with Section 1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 and Section 4 of the Administrative Order No. 17 by procuring common-use supplies and equipment from PS-DBM. Likewise, to secure PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks before procuring common-use supplies from outside suppliers to avoid issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts of the Municipality to present the correct amount of Loans Payable - Domestic account and the expenses in the financial statements.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances on time, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Require the Municipal Accountant to provide certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books in compliance with the documentary requirements required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
Instil to all LGU officials and employees the necessity of submitting complete and proper documentation for cash advances on travel and ensure that documentary requirements for liquidation are properly complied with pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to locate the documents pertaining to non-moving accounts in order to substantiate its balances and to enforce collections. In case of non-collection, request for write-off of the dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to exert more effort to locate the pertinent documents relative to the eleven (11) cows in the total amount of P 110,000.00 for verification and to support any adjustments in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended the Management to:
Secure immediately property insurance for the non-insured government assets; and
Submit the updated Property Inventory Form (PIF) as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage using the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 for all insurable assets not later than April 30 of each year.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to create Disposal Committee to take care of the unserviceable assets and to dispose said items using appropriate mode of disposal, to avoid further deterioration and loss of its salvage value in accordance with Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means for speedy titling of land and secure Certificate of Title in order to protect the interest of the Municipality for any adverse claims by third parties.

We recommended that Management give priority in implementing projects under the 20% Development Fund to attain a desirable socio-economic development and environment outcome for the general public in accordance with JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
We also recommended the Management to include only programs, projects and activities in accordance with the provision of Section 3 of JMC 2017-1 in order to attain the fund’s purpose of providing socio-economic welfare to its constituents

We recommended that Management give priority in implementing projects under the GAD plan and programs to attain a desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for the general public in accordance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-001.
We also recommended the Management to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 in the submission of the required reports to the Audit Team for timely review and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents on terminal leave benefit claims before effecting payments pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the rules and regulations before granting any allowances. Further, we recommended the Management to stop giving monthly transportation allowance to officers and Barangay Presidents of the Federation of Senior Citizen Association considering that said expenditure is not included in the provision as provided under Section 3, Article 21, Rule VI of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9994 and to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers (DVs) as listed in Annex G in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the drivers concerned and agency personnel of the Municipality to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels and submit the same to the Auditor for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that every Purchase Order is duly filled up with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in complying with the terms and conditions thereof in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended the Management to:
Comply with Government Procurement Reform Act, which states that all procurement should be within the approved budget and only projects/procurement included in the APP shall be undertaken;
Limit expenditures to what is appropriated in the budget to avoid utilizing appropriations intended for other programs and or activities and submit explanation/justification on why the monoblock chairs were purchased despite the non-inclusion in the APP as pursuant to Section 7, Article II of RA 9184; and
Carefully plan its APP in order to minimize or avoid deviations from the approved APP and to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 15, 2009 and classify the expenses to proper expenses account. We also recommended to require the Municipal Budget Officer to charge/obligate expenses to its proper account and to stop charging expenses which are not aligned with the appropriation corresponding to its expense account for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 54.1 of Republic Act 9184 and Administrative Order No. 17 to ensure that the procurement of goods and services are obtained in the process which are most advantageous to the Municipality; and
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply strictly with Section 1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 and Section 4 of the Administrative Order No. 17 by procuring common-use supplies and equipment from PS-DBM. Likewise, to secure PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks before procuring common-use supplies from outside suppliers to avoid issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive give prior importance in providing Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in the Municipality and provide sanitary landfill in the disposal of collected wastes in compliance with the provisions of R.A. 9003 in order to have a pollution and health-hazard free environment. We also recommended that Management enjoin the municipal and barangay officials in the enforcement of segregation of wastes at source.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to continue and intensify their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOP’s and notice of delinquency to taxpayers to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.
If circumstances warrant, impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes to satisfy taxes, penalties and costs with the following courses of action:
Seizure of personal property of the taxpayer and sale thereof at public auction;
File civil suit with the proper court; and
Levy on real property and sale of delinquent property or a usable portion thereof at public auction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to verify the journal entries relative to the projects implemented under Trust Fund and to draw the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reflect the correct amount of Water Supply System and Road Networks accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The audit recommendation was implemented by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to assign another employee to collect business taxes in order to ensure check and balance in the custody of cash account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The audit recommendation was effected by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe the procedures in the establishment, utilization and replenishment of the Petty Cash Fund including the correct accounting entries for setting-up and in the replenishment of the PCF.  We also recommended that the setting up of the Petty Cash Fund at the beginning of the year shall be supported by Obligations Request as provided in Section 4.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The audit recommendation was observed by the management. Petty Cash Fund is being maintained through the Imprest Fund System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payroll of Job Order (JOs) personnel are duly supported by accomplishment reports and duly approved daily time records to support their claims for services rendered.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The audit recommendation had been implemented by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretary to submit the required supporting documents and to ensure that all procurement of infrastructure, goods and services are supported by BAC Resolution.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation, duly certified and signed by officials concerned, and payrolls signed by payees.

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds are collected or received before payment is made in order to avoid payment in advance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Some disbursement vouchers are paid despite of incomplete documentary requirements needed to support the propriety and validity of claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to submit all the required supporting documents and all Project contracts be submitted with complete supporting documents within five (5) days from perfection thereof.  Likewise, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the original disbursement vouchers, JEV and official receipts, and ensure that payments made thereto are supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Some contracts for infrastructure projects and goods were paid despite of incomplete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, and the Municipal Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and submit through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in pursuance to Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The audit recommendation was effected by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the remaining unexpended balance of the LDRRMF under the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five years.  In addition, the accounting entries provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 shall be followed in the transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Management executed the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent of the IRA in the municipality’s Annual Budget for the implementation of programs, activities, projects of LCPC in compliance with Section 15 of Chapter I, Title III of R.A. 9344 to address juvenile delinquency in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The municipality allocated 1% of its IRA allocation for programs of Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to verify the journal entries relative to the
projects implemented under Trust Fund and to draw the necessary
adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reflect the correct amount
of Water Supply System and Road Networks accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that Management require the accountable
officer to immediately liquidate/settle his cash advances and to strictly
observe the provisions in the granting and liquidation of cash advances
as provided under PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to assign another employee to collect business
taxes in order to ensure check and balance in the custody of cash
account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe the
procedures in the establishment, utilization and replenishment of the
Petty Cash Fund including the correct accounting entries for setting-up
and in the replenishment of the PCF. We also recommended that the
setting up of the Petty Cash Fund at the beginning of the year shall be
supported by Obligations Request as provided in Section 4.3.1 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Agriculture Officer to exert more effort to locate the
pertinent documents relative to the 11 cows for verification and
adjustments could be made in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that payroll of Job Order (JOs)
personnel are duly supported by accomplishment reports and duly
approved daily time records to support their claims for services
rendered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the drivers
concerned and agency personnel of the Municipality to prepare the
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official
Travels and submit the same to the Auditor for verification to ensure
that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively
controlled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC
Chairman and BAC Secretary to submit the required supporting
documents and to ensure that all procurement of infrastructure, goods
and services are supported by BAC Resolution. We also recommended
that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursement vouchers are
duly supported with complete documentation, duly certified and signed
by officials concerned, and payrolls signed by payees.

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds are collected or received before
payment is made in order to avoid payment in advance.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman
and BAC Secretariat to submit all the required supporting documents
and all Project contracts be submitted with complete supporting
documents within five (5) days from perfection thereof. Likewise, we
recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
submit the original disbursement vouchers, JEV and official receipts,
and ensure that payments made thereto are supported with complete
documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive give prior
importance in providing Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in the
Municipality and provide sanitary landfill in the disposal of collected
wastes in compliance with the provisions of R.A. 9003 in order to have
a pollution and health-hazard free environment. We also
recommended that Management enjoin the municipal and barangay
officials in the enforcement of segregation of wastes at source.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain a
separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, and
the Municipal Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF and submit through the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month in pursuance to Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
remaining unexpended balance of the LDRRMF under the General
Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books for the
purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management
activities of the LDRRMC within the next five years. In addition, the
accounting entries provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 shall be
followed in the transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF-MOOE
to the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct: a)
the Municipal Budget Officer to provide 5% of the total budget for the
accomplishment of Gender and Development; and b) the GAD Focal
Point/Person to review and verify the total amount of GAD Plan and
Budget before submission to the oversight agencies, and submit GAD
Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the
Audit Team on due date as required under Item V of COA Circular No.
2014-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent of the IRA in the
municipality’s Annual Budget for the implementation of programs,
activities, projects of LCPC in compliance with Section 15 of Chapter
I, Title III of R.A. 9344 to address juvenile delinquency in the
Municipality.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means
to exert effect collection of receivables. In case of non-collection, the
agency shall make a request for writing-off of the above receivables
thru the following procedures:

a) The Municipal Accountant shall make a recommendation addressed to the
Local Chief Executive, who, in turn, shall forward the request for writing off
to the Sanguniang Bayan for the adoption of a resolution. The request shall be
supported with documents evidencing action taken/effort exerted to establish
validity and existence of the accounts.
b) The request together with the SB Resolution shall be forwarded by the Local
Chief Executive to the Commission on Audit for review and for approval.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to collect the delinquent Real
Property Tax and market stall rentals in order to generate more income
which could be used in the implementation of development
programs/projects beneficial to the constituents. Management may also
consider the remedies as prescribed under Section 254 of Republic Act
No. 7160 in order to enforce collection of delinquent Real Property Tax.

We have recommended that
the Local Chief Executive
require the Inventory
Committee to conduct
physical inventory of all
Property, Plant and
Equipment of the
municipality in the Special
Education Fund including
Construction in Progress
under the General Fund to
determine their condition and
existence. We also
recommended that the Local
Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to keep
complete and updated
property cards and property,
plant and equipment ledger
cards or subsidiary ledger
cards which should reconcile
with the corresponding
control account of the
Property, Plant and
Equipment account in the
general ledger.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management enjoin the municipal and barangay officials in enforcing the segregation of wastes and consider to provide sanitary landfill in the disposal of collected wastes in compliance with the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in order to have a pollution and health hazard-free environment.

observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s Annual audit Report under Finding No. 9.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality in the Special Education Fund including Construction in Progress under the General Fund to determine their condition and existence. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to keep complete and updated property cards and property, plant and equipment ledger cards or subsidiary ledger cards which should reconcile with the corresponding control account of the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the general ledger.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the journal entries made relative to the constructions undertaken and equipment purchased under the Trust Fund and to make the necessary adjusting entries in the Trust Fund and General Fund for fair presentation of the asset and equity accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the land account to determine the details including information on whether it has a land title or tax declaration. We also recommended that management exert effort to facilitate the titling of ownership on the remaining eight (8) lots in accordance with the provision of Section 449 of GAAM to avoid legal problems in the future. Further, we recommended that appraisal report be submitted by the Appraisal Committee created in determining the book value of the Land account to support the adjusting entries made.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant to adhere to the above-mentioned provision of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A and PD 1445 and attach supporting documents in the payroll to substantiate the claims including the overtime pay.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance to the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 103 that claims for RATA be supported with a certification showing the number of workdays of actual performance in a month issued by the Office of the HRMO for officials covered under Section 7.1 of LBC No. 103, and by Sanggunian Secretary for the members of Sanggunian Bayan covered by Section 7.5 of the same Circular.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust possible means to effect collection of the Receivables by sending notices of collection to debtors in order to generate additional funds to finance other programs of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Local Chie Executive require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the Receivables account to determine the details on the identity of individual and nature of the account to facilitate collection of the account.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Agriculturist to exert effort in finding the pertinent documents relative to the dead cows for verification and adjustment in the books of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the head of the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the regulation in the prioritization and utilization of the SEF particularly for the construction and repair of school buildings and establishment of new extension classes or acquisition of books and instructional materials of students and teachers as required in DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and 1A and in consonance with Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 in order to ensure an effective educational development within the locality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive give prior importance in providing Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and consider to provide sanitary landfill in the disposal of collected wastes in compliance with the provisions of R.A. 9003 in order to have a pollution and health-hazard free environment. We also recommended that the local government unit enjoin the municipal and barangay officials in the enforcement of segregation of wastes at source.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Contractor concerned to refund the amount of P51,932.41 pertaining to the costs of uninstalled items and exterior paint already fading as per Inspection Report dated November 14, 2016.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging expenses under GAD Fund which are not related in addressing gender issues, promote women’s empowerment and gender equality as provided in the provisions of the Joint Circular of PCW-NEDA-DBM; and to charge it to the regular Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the LGU.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and prepare and submit through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, respectively, in pursuance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the IRA for the implementation of programs on child delinquency of the LCPC in compliance with the provision of Section 15 of Chapter I, Title III of R.A. 9344. We also recommended that programs and activities funded therefrom should focus towards child protection and ensure proper implementation of programs in order to prevent juvenile delinquency.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify/improve their collection strategies on taxes due and collectibles by sending RPTOP and notice of delinquency to taxpayers; and to conduct information campaign to improve collections; and if warranted, enforce the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes.
We have recommended that management continue to deduct and remit all taxes withheld due the BIR.

We have recommended that the Accounting Division verify the journal entries made relative to the Installation of Materials for Electronic Business Permit and Licensing System as well as the cost of furniture and equipment and make the necessary correcting entries in the books for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented 2015

AAR Physical count 2015 of Property, Plant and Equipment was conducted but did not include Construction in Progress under General Fund. PPE under Special Education Fund.

Partially Implemented. Reiterated in this year’s Annual Audit Report under Finding No. 1.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s Annual Audit Report under Finding No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant maintain the petty cash fund under the imprest system and in accordance with the procedures in the establishment, utilization and recording as provided in Section 4.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 48, Vol. I of MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management increase the amount of fidelity bonds of the Municipal Treasurer and the Local Revenue Collection Officer II in accordance with Item 5.1 of the Treasury Circular to sufficiently cover their cash accountabilities for a given period.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management strictly comply with the requirements stated in the provisions of COA Circular No. 98-002 before engaging the services of a private lawyer. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the monthly accomplishment report of the Legal Consultant to support the claim of honorarium.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat submit all the required documents and that all Project Contracts be submitted with complete supporting documents within five (5) days from perfection thereof. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments made thereto be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented 2015

observations.recommendation: We commended the local government unit for allocating at least 5% of the total LGU Budget for GAD PPAs and prepared GAD Plan and Budget. On the other hand, we recommended that management require the Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to oversight agencies on due date as required under Item 7-C6 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented 2015
